sections.paragraph:
-

(1) id. 1Sa 17:5, 38 1Sa 17:38, pl. שִׁרְיֹנִים Neh 4:10, שִׁרְיֹנֹות 2Ch 26:14. (Syr. ܫܶܪܝܳܢܐܳ id.) The same is סִרְיֹון, which see.

(2) [Sirion], pr.n. given to Mount Hermon by the Sidonians, Deu 3:9, compare שְׂנִיר. This name appears to have been taken from its resemblance to a breastplate, just like the Gr. Θωράξ, for the mountain of Magnesia.
